lines.english: Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਅੰਕਾਰਿ ਏਕ ਧੁਨਿ ਏਕੈ ਏਕੈ ਰਾਗੁ ਅਲਾਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: oankaar ek dhun ekai ekai raag alaapai |
lines.english: He sings the song of the One Universal Creator; he sings the tune of the One Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕਾ ਦੇਸੀ ਏਕੁ ਦਿਖਾਵੈ ਏਕੋ ਰਹਿਆ ਬਿਆਪੈ ॥
lines.translit: ekaa desee ek dikhaavai eko rahiaa biaapai |
lines.english: He lives in the land of the One Lord, shows the way to the One Lord, and remains attuned to the One Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕਾ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਏਕਾ ਹੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਏਕੋ ਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਜਾਪੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: ekaa surat ekaa hee sevaa eko gur te jaapai |1|
lines.english: He centers his consciousness on the One Lord, and serves only the One Lord, who is known through the Guru. ||1||
Blessed and good is such a kirtanee, who sings such Praises.

lines.gurmukhi: ਛੋਡਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਕੇ ਧੰਧ ਸੁਆਉ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: chhodd maaeaa ke dhandh suaau |1| rahaau |
lines.english: and renounces the entanglements and pursuits of Maya. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਚ ਬਜਿਤ੍ਰ ਕਰੇ ਸੰਤੋਖਾ ਸਾਤ ਸੁਰਾ ਲੈ ਚਾਲੈ ॥
lines.translit: panch bajitr kare santokhaa saat suraa lai chaalai |
lines.english: He makes the five virtues, like contentment, his musical instruments, and plays the seven notes of the love of the Lord.
He sings the Glorious Praises of the Lord,

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਜਾ ਮਾਣੁ ਤਾਣੁ ਤਜਿ ਤਾਨਾ ਪਾਉ ਨ ਬੀਗਾ ਘਾਲੈ ॥
lines.translit: baajaa maan taan taj taanaa paau na beegaa ghaalai |
lines.english: The notes he plays are the renunciation of pride and power; his feet keep the beat on the straight path.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫੇਰੀ ਫੇਰੁ ਨ ਹੋਵੈ ਕਬ ਹੀ ਏਕੁ ਸਬਦੁ ਬੰਧਿ ਪਾਲੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: feree fer na hovai kab hee ek sabad bandh paalai |2|
lines.english: He does not enter the cycle of reincarnation ever again; he keeps the One Word of the Shabad tied to the hem of his robe. ||2||
The dramatic gestures of acting are celestial bliss.
The tinkling of the ankle bells is the shedding of sorrows and worries.
To play like Naarad, is to know that the Lord is ever-present.
If anyone, out of millions of people, becomes pleasing to his Lord and Master,
He sings the Lord's Praises in this way.
Such a dancer is not reincarnated again. ||3||
I have taken the Support of the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy.
Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:
Says Nanak, the Kirtan of the One Lord's Praises are sung there. ||4||8||
Some serve Him as 'Gusain', others as 'Allaah'. ||1||
He is the Cause of causes, the Generous Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਈ ਬੋਲੈ ਰਾਮ ਰਾਮ ਕੋਈ ਖੁਦਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: koee bolai raam raam koee khudaae |
lines.english: Some call Him, 'Raam, Raam', and some call Him, 'Khudaa-i'.
He showers His Grace and Mercy upon us. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਈ ਨਾਵੈ ਤੀਰਥਿ ਕੋਈ ਹਜ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: koee naavai teerath koee haj jaae |
lines.english: Some bathe at sacred shrines of pilgrimage, and some make the pilgrimage to Mecca.|

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਈ ਕਰੈ ਪੂਜਾ ਕੋਈ ਸਿਰੁ ਨਿਵਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: koee karai poojaa koee sir nivaae |2|
lines.english: Some perform devotional worship services, and some bow their heads in prayer. ||2||
Some wear blue robes, and some wear white. ||3||
Some read the Vedas, and some the Koran.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਈ ਕਹੈ ਤੁਰਕੁ ਕੋਈ ਕਹੈ ਹਿੰਦੂ ॥
lines.translit: koee kahai turak koee kahai hindoo |
lines.english: Some call themselves Muslim, and some call themselves Hindu.
Says Nanak, one who realizes the Hukam of God's Will,

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਸਾਹਿਬ ਕਾ ਤਿਨਿ ਭੇਦੁ ਜਾਤਾ ॥੫॥੯॥
lines.translit: prabh saahib kaa tin bhed jaataa |5|9|
lines.english: knows the secrets of his Lord and Master. ||5||9||
Some yearn for paradise, and others long for heaven. ||4||
The wind merges into the wind.
Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:
The light blends into the light.
Who has died? O, who has died?
The dust becomes one with the dust.
What support is there for the one who is lamenting? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗਿਆਨੀ ਮਿਲਿ ਕਰਹੁ ਬੀਚਾਰਾ ਇਹੁ ਤਉ ਚਲਤੁ ਭਇਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: braham giaanee mil karahu beechaaraa ihu tau chalat bheaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: O God-realized beings, meet together and consider this. What a wondrous thing has happened! ||1||Pause||
No one knows what happens after death.
Mortal beings are bound by the bonds of doubt and attachment.
The Creator Lord created this creation.
The one who is lamenting will also arise and depart.
When life becomes a dream, the blind man babbles and grieves in vain. ||2||
The soul does not perish; it is imperishable. ||3||
It comes and goes, subject to the Will of the Infinite Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਹ ਕੋ ਮੂਆ ਨ ਮਰਣੈ ਜੋਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: neh ko mooaa na maranai jog |
lines.english: No one dies; no one is capable of dying.
I am a sacrifice to the one who knows this.
Says Nanak, the Guru has dispelled my doubt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਇਹੁ ਜਾਣਹੁ ਸੋ ਇਹੁ ਨਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: jo ihu jaanahu so ihu naeh |
lines.english: That which is known, does not exist.
Meditate on the Lord of the Universe, the Beloved Lord of the World.
Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਕੋਈ ਮਰੈ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਜਾਇਆ ॥੪॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: naa koee marai na aavai jaaeaa |4|10|
lines.english: No one dies; no one comes or goes. ||4||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਸਿਮਰਿ ਤੂ ਜੀਵਹਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਨ ਖਾਈ ਮਹਾ ਕਾਲੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: raam naam simar too jeeveh fir na khaaee mahaa kaal |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Meditating in remembrance on the Lord's Name, you shall live, and the Great Death shall not consume you ever again. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਟਿ ਜਨਮ ਭ੍ਰਮਿ ਭ੍ਰਮਿ ਭ੍ਰਮਿ ਆਇਓ ॥
lines.translit: kott janam bhram bhram bhram aaeo |
lines.english: Through millions of incarnations, you have come, wandering, wandering, wandering.
